response.justification: Maria is a credible staff-level generalist with 16+ years of engineering experience, demonstrated TypeScript/Node.js depth, and some genuinely relevant AI-adjacent work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling in her current role). However, the role requires 8+ years building production AI solutions, and her AI exposure is almost entirely confined to a single role started in April 2025. Python — the dominant language for AI/ML engineering — is absent from her skills list. There is no evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI), which are either required or strongly preferred. Her cloud experience is limited to AWS CDK in a pricing-team context rather than AI system delivery. The gap between her current trajectory and the seniority floor for this role is too wide to bridge in an interview.
strengths: Genuine staff-level engineering track record across multiple companies with polyglot, full-stack depth
Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling experience — directly listed as a key technology in the JD
Proven ability to own high-throughput, data-intensive systems (Dunnhumby micro-services at millions of req/s)
concerns: Python is absent from the skills list, which is a critical gap for production AI/ML engineering at this level
AI and agentic experience is limited to ~1 year in the current role — far below the 8-year minimum requirement
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation/observability, or multi-agent frameworks
